articles.description: BSE shares rose sharply on Tuesday on expectations of replacing Wipro in the Nifty50 during the September rejig. The rally comes after strong Q4 results and bullish brokerage views. Analysts remain divided on the stock, with Nuvama staying positive, Jefferies cautious, and Motilal Oswal turning neutral but raising its target price.

The newly launched Elite, Elite Plus, and Legacy Savings Accounts feature a range of value-added benefits, including premium debit cards and a thoughtfully curated selection of exclusive lifestyle ... articles.title: Wealthtech platform Trackk raises $3.7 million led by Lightspeed Ventures
articles.description: Trackk, a stock trading platform for Gen Z investors, has secured $3.7 million in a funding round. Lightspeed Ventures led the investment. The platform uses AI for stock discovery and simplified trading. Trackk aims to expand its product offerings and marketing efforts with this capital.

articles.description: A 20-year-old student was killed in a hit-and-run in southwest Delhi; police are investigating the incident and reviewing CCTV footage.

articles.title: Karnataka: Davanagere Headmaster Dies After Heart Attack Triggered During Lightning Storm
articles.description: A school headmaster in Karnataka’s Davanagere district died after suffering a heart attack reportedly triggered during a thunderstorm. The incident occurred when he was standing outside his house during heavy rain and a loud thunderclap startled him.

articles.description: Clay or earthen pots naturally absorb excess moisture, helping the curd set firmer and thicker.

articles.title: BJP MLA Gets Himself Fined After Viral Video Shows Him Riding Bike Without Helmet
articles.description: BJP MLA Shalabh Mani Tripathi from Uttar Pradesh’s Deoria district was fined Rs 1,000 after a viral video showed him riding a motorcycle without a helmet. The MLA later admitted his mistake and personally requested police to issue a challan against him. He said he rode the bike after a party worker insisted he “inaugurate” the new motorcycle.

articles.title: West Bengal raises age limit for government jobs by 5 years, widens entry for aspirants
Bike rally in Bengaluru to raise awareness on skin donation
Discarding of clothes in Tamirabharani at Papanasam continues unabatedly; Collector inspects ‘pollution point’
India sets $2 trillion export target in 5 years for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'
articles.description: Union Minister Piyush Goyal set a $2 trillion export target for the next five years under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative. He launched the Bharat Vyapar Mahotsav to scale up indigenous goods and strengthen India's global market presence.

articles.title: POCSO case: Delhi HC notice to school staffer on police bail plea
articles.description: The Delhi HC issued a notice to a school caretaker accused of assaulting a 3-year-old, following a Delhi Police plea challenging his bail. The police argued the POCSO case is a grave offence and the trial court erred in granting bail.

articles.title: Greater Noida: Woman falls to death; husband, father-in-law held
articles.description: A woman died under suspicious circumstances after falling from the rooftop of her in-laws' house in Greater Noida. Her husband and father-in-law have been arrested after her family alleged dowry harassment, torture, and murder.

articles.title: NSE Commences Trading In Electronic Gold Receipts Segment: What's This?
articles.description: The exchange says live trading in EGRs began from Monday, May 18, after a successful mock trading session conducted on May 16 without any system issues or exceptions.

articles.title: Mouni Roy's husband Suraj Nambiar denies 'malicious' separation claims
articles.description: Suraj Nambiar denies rumours of alimony disputes and third-party involvement in his separation from Mouni Roy. He called the reports 'malicious' and stated the decision to part ways was mutual, amicable, and with respect for each other.

articles.title: MotoGP: Di Giannantonio wins dramatic Catalan GP after major crashes
articles.description: Fabio Di Giannantonio won a chaotic, red-flagged Catalan GP, his first victory since 2023. The race was marred by high-profile crashes from Jorge Martin, Pedro Acosta, Alex Marquez, and Johann Zarco, leading to multiple restarts.

articles.title: Offer namaz in shifts or we'll use other methods, says Yogi Adityanath
articles.description: UP CM Yogi Adityanath stated that namaz must be offered in a regulated manner, suggesting shifts if space is limited. He warned that compliance for public order will be sought through persuasion, and if that fails, 'other methods' will be used.

articles.title: PM Modi's historic Norway visit to boost India-Nordic relations
articles.description: PM Modi arrived in Oslo for a historic visit, the first by an Indian PM to Norway in over four decades. Welcomed by PM Jonas Gahr Støre, the visit aims to boost geopolitical and economic ties, especially after the recent EFTA agreement.

articles.title: Lakhimpur Kheri accident: PM Modi announces Rs 2 lakh ex-gratia
articles.description: A tragic road accident in UP's Lakhimpur Kheri claimed 10 lives. PM Modi announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh for the deceased's families and Rs 50,000 for the injured. CM Yogi Adityanath also expressed condolences and ordered assistance.

articles.title: PM condoles UP accident; announces ex-gratia
articles.description: Lakhimpur Kheri: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday expressed deep grief over the loss of 10 lives in a tragic road accident in Uttar Pradesh's Lakhimpur Kheri and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh each for the next of kin of the deceased.The Prime Minister also announced financial assistance of Rs 50,000 for those injured in the accident.In a post on X, the Prime Minister's Office stated, "Deeply pained to hear about a mishap in Lakhimpur Kheri, Uttar Pradesh. My condolences to those who have lost their loved ones. May the injured recover at the earliest. An ex-gratia of Rs. 2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of each deceased. The injured would be given Rs. 50,000."Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath also condoled the Lakhimpur Kheri accident, terming the incident "heartbreaking and soul-wrenching.The Chief Minister further stated that instructions have been issued to the local administration to provide prompt and adequate assistance to the injured."The loss of lives in the unfortunate road accident in Lakhimpur Kheri district is extremely heartbreaking and soul-wrenching. My condolences are with the bereaved families. Instructions have been issued to the local administration to provide prompt and adequate assistance to the injured and the affected. I pray to Lord Shri Ram that the departed souls find a place at His divine feet and the injured recover swiftly," said CM Yogi.A road accident occurred at around 7:00 AM on Monday, which resulted in the death of 10 people. The incident took place near Barheta village within the jurisdictional limits of the Isa Nagar police station, where a truck and van collided head-on on the Lakhimpur-Bahraich Highway.The accident took place at around 7 am on Monday near Barheta village under the jurisdiction of the Isa Nagar police station on the Lakhimpur-Bahraich Highway, when a truck and a van collided head-on."Today at 7 AM, a road accident took place near Barheta village within the jurisdictional limits of the Isa Nagar police station on the Lakhimpur-Bahraich highway, where a collision occurred between a truck and a van carrying 10 people," said Lakhimpur Kheri CO Shamsher Bahadur Singh.According to local authorities, the injured were rushed to a nearby hospital for treatment, while the bodies of the deceased have been sent for post-mortem examination.Further investigation into the incident is underway.
